OPP-01_OPP-02_combined.opportunity: Your Ball Matters + Fair Price (combined Tier 1 campaign)

leading_indicators.metric: Email open rate on launch sequence
leading_indicators.benchmark: PG historical average for product launches (likely 25-35%)
leading_indicators.signal: Subject line resonance — does 'your ball matters' framing generate curiosity?

leading_indicators.metric: Click-through rate from email to product page
leading_indicators.signal: Offer relevance — does the audience care enough to look?

leading_indicators.metric: Product page scroll depth / time on page
leading_indicators.benchmark: PG historical average for product pages
leading_indicators.signal: Content engagement — is the distance + proof messaging holding attention?
Purchase intent — does the proof + price combination convert interest to action?
Which angle resonates: distance-lead vs. price-lead vs. proof-lead?

lagging_indicators.benchmark: PG historical for product launch emails
lagging_indicators.signal: Email list monetization for consumable vs. one-time device products

lagging_indicators.benchmark: Golf ball industry repeat rate (~30-40% within 6 months for liked balls)
lagging_indicators.signal: Product-market fit — does the on-course experience match the marketing promise?

lagging_indicators.benchmark: Must be recoverable within 2 purchases given ball margins
lagging_indicators.signal: Campaign efficiency — can iON+ acquire customers profitably?

lagging_indicators.metric: Net Promoter Score / customer feedback sentiment
lagging_indicators.signal: Word-of-mouth potential — will buyers recommend to playing partners?

decision_triggers.action: Revise subject lines and preview text. Test 'your ball matters' vs. direct distance claim vs. price-forward framing.
decision_triggers.escalation: If CTR stays < 2% after revision, the frame claim may not resonate with PG's list — test with cold traffic instead.

decision_triggers.trigger: Product page add-to-cart rate < 3% after 1,000 visits
decision_triggers.action: Audit page structure. Check: is the Golf Laboratories data prominent enough? Is the price revealed too early/late? Is the alignment system section helping or confusing?
decision_triggers.escalation: A/B test page variants with different section orders (proof-first vs. feature-first vs. price-first).

decision_triggers.action: Messaging is generating interest but the page isn't converting. Diagnose: landing page mismatch, price objection not handled, trust gap on product page.
decision_triggers.escalation: Add testimonials (if available) or more prominent Golf Laboratories data. Consider video demo.

decision_triggers.action: Product experience may not match marketing promise. Survey first-time buyers: did distance improve? Did they like the feel? Did they use the alignment system?
decision_triggers.escalation: If the product isn't delivering on the promise, the marketing is ahead of the product. Pause scaling and address product feedback.

decision_triggers.trigger: Distance-lead ads outperform price-lead ads by 2x+ CTR
decision_triggers.action: Confirm OPP-01 as primary angle. Shift budget allocation toward distance messaging. Use price as the closing argument, not the opener.
decision_triggers.escalation: None — this is the expected outcome based on research.

kill_criteria.criterion: Fewer than 100 units sold in first 30 days to PG email list
kill_criteria.action: PG's warmest audience isn't converting. This suggests either (a) product-market mismatch with existing PG customers, or (b) fundamental messaging failure. Pause campaign, conduct customer interviews.
kill_criteria.severity: HIGH — if PG's own list won't buy, cold traffic is unlikely to perform.

kill_criteria.criterion: CAC exceeds $30 after 60 days of ad optimization
kill_criteria.action: Acquisition cost is too high for a $30 consumable product. Either find cheaper acquisition channels (organic, partnerships, YouTube content) or reassess the paid advertising approach.
kill_criteria.severity: MEDIUM — may indicate the product needs more organic/content-driven marketing rather than direct response.

kill_criteria.action: Customers aren't coming back. The product experience isn't meeting expectations set by marketing. This is a product problem, not a marketing problem. Investigate: distance claims vs. on-course reality, feel complaints, alignment system usability.
kill_criteria.severity: HIGH — consumable without repeat purchase is unsustainable.

baseline_benchmarks.golf_ball_market_context: US golf ball market ~$1.2B annually. Titleist holds ~50% premium share. DTC segment growing 15-20% annually. Average golfer buys 4-6 dozen/year.
baseline_benchmarks.pg_context: PG has an email list of active golfers, existing e-commerce infrastructure, and established brand in training/devices. This is their first consumable product — repeat purchase mechanics are new.
baseline_benchmarks.pricing_context: ProV1: $55/dozen. Vice Pro: $26-33/dozen. Maxfli Tour: $27/dozen. Kirkland: $14/dozen. iON+ at $30-35 sits in the middle of DTC range.
baseline_benchmarks.competitive_timing: No major competitor has launched a swing-speed-specific ball at this price point with independent testing. Window is open but will close if the concept works.
